proof_quality.rationale: The proof summary is well-constructed. Exhaustiveness is evident: the agent searched the 1950 U.S. Federal Census (primary evidence for three children), Ruth Tolley Zabriskie's 2008 obituary (independent secondary source corroborating all four children), Find a Grave and BillionGraves cemetery indexes (Patricia Ann), and explicitly documented search gaps and pursued-but-unavailable resources (1960 census closed until 2032, Kenneth's 1985 obituary coverage gap for Utah newspapers 1984–1990, Utah birth certificates under 100-year privacy restriction). No conflicts required resolution; sources aligned consistently. Corroboration is independent: the 1950 census (primary, enumerator-recorded household) and the 2008 obituary (secondary, family-provided information) are different record types, created decades apart, by different hands, and constitute independent evidence. Tier is appropriate: 'probable' is justified for Terrie, Joleen, and Patricia (two independent source types each) but appropriately cautious about Ken Zabriskie (single source, though the proof acknowledges that the inaccessible 1960 census would strengthen it to 'proved'). The narrative demonstrates restraint and methodological soundness, explicitly noting which children have dual corroboration and which do not.

entry.rationale: Kenneth Zabriskie died 31 January 1985 in Nephi, Juab, Utah. His obituary, published shortly after death, is the most likely single source to name all surviving children by name and spouse. FamilySearch collection 'Utah, Obituaries from Utah Newspapers, 1850-2005' (id 2302011) is indexed and covers this period. This is the highest-yield item for a completeness question about children.

Two 1950 census records for Kenneth Zabriskie are already identified in the tree (ARKs 6XKM-FRX3 and 6XKM-F3MQ). The 1950 census, taken 1 April 1950, is four and a half years after the September 1945 marriage and would enumerate any children born in that interval. Extracting these records will establish which children were present in the household by 1950.

Four LDS Church Census records for Kenneth Zabriskie spanning 1950-1960 are already identified in the tree (ARKs DX32-9K2M/1950, ZRCK-RCN2/1950, DX32-92W2/1955, DXS1-CDT2/1960). Church census records list household members by name and relationship, providing annual snapshots that would capture children as they were born throughout the decade. These complement the 1950 U.S. Census and are particularly important for children born after 1950.

The 1960 U.S. Census would show the Zabriskie household ten years after 1950, capturing any children born in the intervening decade. Patricia Zabriskie, identified as a deceased daughter in Ruth's 2008 obituary, may have been born in the early-to-mid 1950s and would appear here. FamilySearch holds the indexed 1960 census.

Ruth Tolley Zabriskie's 2008 obituary (Deseret News, 17 Jul 2008) is already partially preserved as a LifeSketch in the tree, naming children Terrie (Allan) Kay, Joleen Alverson, Ken (Melinda) Zabriskie, and deceased Patricia Zabriskie. Extracting the full obituary as a formal research.json source with assertions provides the foundational documentary evidence list of children against which all other sources should be compared.

Ruth's 2008 obituary states Patricia Zabriskie preceded her in death. A Find a Grave, BillionGraves, or Vine Bluff Cemetery record for Patricia would establish her birth and death dates, confirming her existence as a child of the couple and providing context for completeness. Kenneth's own Find a Grave index (9Z2H-89X) and BillionGraves (9Z73-Y5G) entries already in the tree should also be extracted — memorial pages sometimes list family members.

Utah birth records for children of Kenneth and Ruth Zabriskie would directly verify parentage for each child. FamilySearch collection 'Utah, State Archives, Records, 1848-2001' (id 2001084) and the Utah Death Certificates 1904-1966 (id 1747615) may cover births/deaths of children. Search for Terrie, Joleen, Ken, and Patricia Zabriskie born ca. 1946-1965 in Juab or adjacent Utah counties.

Kenneth Zabriskie's 1985 death certificate (Utah) would list informant-provided data on survivors, potentially naming children. Utah death certificates from 1985 fall outside the indexed FamilySearch collection (which ends in 1966) but may be accessible through the Utah Division of Archives and Records Service (archives.utah.gov) or the Utah State Archives collection (id 2001084). As an independent record created close to Kenneth's death, it provides corroborating evidence for the children list.

Survived by her children, Terrie (Allan) Kay of Mona; Joleen Alverson of Spanish Fork; Ken (Melinda) Zabriskie of Draper; 13 grandchildren and 36 great-grand-children. Preceded in death by her parents, her husband Kenneth; daughter, Patricia Zabriskie; son-in-law, Curtis Alverson; granddaughter, Misty Olsen and great-grand-daughter, Ashley Gardner; 12 brothers and sisters. Funeral Services will be held at 2 p.m. on Saturday, July 19, 2008 at the Nephi North Stake Center, 1125 North 400 East. entry.narrative_markdown:
## Children of John Kenneth Zabriskie and Ruth Tolley Zabriskie, Nephi, Juab, Utah

**Research Question:** What children are documented for John Kenneth Zabriskie (1916–1985) of Nephi, Juab, Utah, in U.S. census records, Utah vital records, obituaries, and other contemporary sources?

**Tier: Probable | Form: Summary**

---

John Kenneth Zabriskie (b. 30 October 1916, Mt. Pleasant, Sanpete, Utah; d. 31 January 1985, Nephi, Juab, Utah) and his wife Ruth Tolley Zabriskie (b. 8 August 1922, Nephi, Utah; d. 15 July 2008, Nephi, Utah; married September 1945) are documented by two independent source types as the parents of four children: Terrie Lee, Joleen, Patricia Ann, and Ken Zabriskie.

### 1950 U.S. Federal Census

The household of “Kenith J Zabriskie” — a documented spelling variant of John Kenneth Zabriskie — enumerated at Nephi, Juab, Utah, on 1 April 1950, included wife “Ruth T Zabriskie” and three daughters: **Terrie Lee Zabriskie** (b. ca. 1945, Utah), **Joleen Zabriskie** (b. ca. 1947, Utah), and **Patricia Ann Zabriskie** (b. ca. 1949, Utah). The enumerator’s direct recording of the household constitutes primary information; the household-member relationship designations are direct evidence of parentage. The FamilySearch derivative index (ark:/61903/1:1:6XKM-FRX3) was accessed; the original population schedule image was not directly examined. Source classification: derivative source, primary information, direct evidence. [a_002–a_007]

### Ruth Tolley Zabriskie 2008 Obituary

The Deseret News obituary of Ruth Tolley Zabriskie, published 17 July 2008 (Salt Lake City, Utah), names four children: “Terrie (Allan) Kay of Mona” (daughter), “Joleen Alverson of Spanish Fork” (daughter), “Ken (Melinda) Zabriskie of Draper” (son), and “Patricia Zabriskie” (daughter, preceded in death). The obituary corroborates all three daughters named in the 1950 census and uniquely documents Ken Zabriskie, born after the April 1950 census date. Source classification: authored source, secondary information, direct evidence for child-of relationship (informant: unnamed family member, family_not_present). [a_015–a_025]

### Cemetery Records — Patricia Ann Zabriskie

The FamilySearch Find a Grave index (ark:/61903/1:1:QVKN-LSD4) and BillionGraves index (ark:/61903/1:1:2HXB-BKJ) both record Patricia Ann Zabriskie born 21 December 1948, died 3 December 1970, buried Vine Bluff Cemetery, Nephi, Juab, Utah. These derivative records confirm her birth year (consistent with the 1950 census age, accounting for normal rounding), her death before her mother (as stated in the obituary), and her connection to Nephi. The two cemetery indexes likely derive from the same gravestone inscription and therefore constitute one evidence unit, not two independent proofs. Source classification: derivative source, secondary information, direct evidence for vital events. [a_026–a_034]

### Source Gaps and Limitations

Kenneth Zabriskie’s own 1985 obituary was not located in any online collection. FamilySearch full-text search confirmed a coverage gap for Utah newspapers ca. 1984–1990; the indexed “Utah, Obituaries from Utah Newspapers, 1850–2005” collection does not extend to 1985. The 1960 U.S. Census is closed until 2032 under the federal 72-year rule. Utah birth certificates for the children (ca. 1945–1965) are subject to a 100-year privacy restriction and are not available online. Kenneth’s 1985 Utah death certificate requires an in-person request to the Utah Division of Archives and Records Service. These are pursued-and-unavailable resources, not unsearched gaps.

### Evidence Weighing and Correlation

Terrie Lee, Joleen, and Patricia Ann are each documented by two independent source types: an original record with primary information (1950 census) and an authored source with secondary information (2008 obituary). Patricia is additionally corroborated by two cemetery indexes. This cross-source convergence — different record types, different informants, different creation dates — provides a strong evidentiary basis for their identification as children of John Kenneth Zabriskie.

Ken Zabriskie presents a different situation: he is documented solely by the 2008 obituary. The 1960 census (which would likely have shown him in the household) is inaccessible until 2032. While the obituary’s description is specific (“Ken (Melinda) Zabriskie of Draper”) and internally consistent with the other documented children, single-source documentation meets only a probable tier under the GPS standard. No record contradicts his inclusion in the family.

No conflicts were identified across any of the sources examined.

### Conclusion

At a **probable** tier, John Kenneth Zabriskie and Ruth Tolley Zabriskie of Nephi, Juab, Utah, had four documented children:

1. **Terrie Lee Zabriskie** (b. ca. 1945, Utah), later known as Terrie Kay (husband: Allan Kay), residing Mona, Juab County, Utah, as of 2008.
2. **Joleen Zabriskie** (b. ca. 1947, Utah), later known as Joleen Alverson (husband: Curtis Alverson), residing Spanish Fork, Utah County, Utah, as of 2008.
3. **Patricia Ann Zabriskie** (b. 21 December 1948; d. 3 December 1970, Nephi, Juab, Utah), buried Vine Bluff Cemetery, Nephi.
4. **Ken Zabriskie** (b. after 1 April 1950), husband of Melinda, residing Draper, Salt Lake County, Utah, as of 2008.

The conclusion could advance to **proved** if independent primary-information records corroborating Ken Zabriskie’s parentage are obtained — most likely the 1960 U.S. Census (available 2032) or Kenneth’s 1985 Utah death certificate via the Utah State Archives.

Inaccessible sources (1960 census unavailable until 2032; Kenneth's 1985 death certificate requires in-person Utah archives request; children's birth records under 100-year privacy restriction; Juab County probate file requires in-person Fourth District Court request) are pursued-and-unavailable, not unsearched gaps.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes. Three independent source types document four children of John Kenneth Zabriskie and Ruth Tolley Zabriskie: (1) Terrie Lee Zabriskie/Kay (b.ca.1945, Utah), (2) Joleen Zabriskie/Alverson (b.ca.1947, Utah), (3) Patricia Ann Zabriskie (b.21 Dec 1948, d.3 Dec 1970, Nephi), and (4) Ken Zabriskie (b.after April 1950). The question is answered at probable tier.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: FamilySearch census, FTS, indexed obituary collection, cemetery indexes (Find a Grave, BillionGraves), LDS Church Census, Utah death records, Utah birth records, Juab County probate (FamilySearch, negative). All identified record types searched. Inaccessible: 1960 census (2032), 1985 death certificate (archives), birth certificates (privacy), probate file (not digitized).
stop_criteria.original_substitution: 1950 census original image not directly examined — FamilySearch derivative index used. Obituary text preserved in tree LifeSketch. Cemetery records are derivative. Limitation noted; does not change conclusion given cross-source consistency.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: Terrie Lee and Joleen: 1950 census (primary) + 2008 obituary (secondary) — two independent source types. Patricia Ann: census + obituary + Find a Grave + BillionGraves — multiple corroborating sources. Ken Zabriskie: obituary only — single source; independent corroboration inaccessible (1960 census closed until 2032; probate not indexed). This limitation is documented and the tier is set accordingly.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: 1950 U.S. Census is an original record with primary information (enumerator witnessed household composition). 2008 obituary is authored with secondary information. Cemetery records are derivative.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: No discrepancies across sources. 1950 census (three daughters) and 2008 obituary (three daughters plus Ken, born after April 1950) are consistent. No conflicts.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low for Terrie, Joleen, Patricia — corroborated by multiple independent sources. Moderate for Ken — single authored source; 1960 census (2032) and death certificate (Utah archives) could provide independent corroboration. Probate records searched on FamilySearch, zero results — not indexed online. No accessible record expected to name a different set of children.
